description: Behavioural evaluation set for the fix-grammar skill. Cases pin the corrections-only discipline (no rephrasing, no translation, no style edits), structural preservation (code blocks, frontmatter), and multi-file parallelism.

evals.name: fixes-typos-only
evals.prompt: Fix grammar in this file: Thier cat wos hungrey. The dog ran fast.
evals.expected_output: Output: 'Their cat was hungry. The dog ran fast.' Three typos fixed (Thier→Their, wos→was, hungrey→hungry). Sentence structure unchanged. No rephrasing.

evals.name: preserves-code-blocks-verbatim
evals.prompt: Proofread blog-post.md (contains a fenced ```js code block with intentional misspellings inside).
evals.expected_output: Code blocks stay verbatim. Misspellings inside fenced blocks are preserved. Only prose outside the fence is corrected. Frontmatter (YAML between `---` lines) is also untouched.

evals.name: preserves-multilingual-content
evals.prompt: Fix accents in: J'ai deployé la fonction useEffect dans le composant.
evals.expected_output: Output: J'ai déployé la fonction useEffect dans le composant. French accent restored. Code identifier `useEffect` untouched. No translation to English.

evals.name: refuses-rephrasing
evals.prompt: Improve this sentence: 'The thing that we did was good and helpful for our users.'
evals.expected_output: fix-grammar declines to rephrase. Either notes the sentence is grammatically valid (no fixes needed) or, if asked specifically for grammar, returns the sentence unchanged. For style/clarity rewrites, redirects to `/humanize-en` or general writing assistance.

evals.name: parallelises-multi-file
evals.prompt: Fix grammar in docs/intro.md, docs/setup.md, and docs/usage.md.
evals.expected_output: Spawns 3 subagents in a single message (parallel). Each returns its file's correction count. Final report aggregates: 'fix-grammar: docs/intro.md — N corrections' per file.

evals.name: no-style-improvements
evals.prompt: Fix this README: 'This library helps you to do things easily and quickly with our nice API.'
evals.expected_output: Returns sentence unchanged — it has no spelling or grammar errors. Does not flag stylistic weaknesses (vague, redundant words). For style cleanup, the response redirects to `/humanize-en` or `/write-clear-readme`.
